title: Process Memory Inspection
description: Agent scans process memory regions to extract runtime data (e.g., credentials, UI state) from other running processes on Windows via Win32 API and YARA pattern matching.

actors.name: Agent/Operator
actors.description: Invokes scanner via CLI (procmem_scanner.py <PID> <pattern> [--mode] [--llm]) or imports scan_memory() directly

actors.name: Windows Kernel (kernel32.dll)
actors.description: Provides OpenProcess, VirtualQueryEx, ReadProcessMemory, CloseHandle WinAPI calls via ctypes.windll.kernel32

actors.name: Target Process
actors.description: The running Windows process identified by PID whose virtual memory is being inspected

actors.name: YARA Engine
actors.description: Compiles and applies pattern-matching rules against raw memory buffers

use_cases.name: Scan Process Memory
use_cases.description: Top-level use case: opens target process, iterates all committed readable memory regions, applies YARA pattern, returns matches with address context

use_cases.name: Build YARA Pattern Rule
use_cases.description: Classifies pattern as hex bytes or ASCII/wide string, compiles a YARA rule object. Auto-detects hex via is_hex_pattern().

use_cases.name: Open Process Handle
use_cases.description: Acquires handle with flags 0x0400|0x0010 (PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION | PROCESS_VM_READ). Fails on system/higher-integrity processes.

use_cases.name: Enumerate Memory Regions
use_cases.description: Iterates 64-bit user-space (0x0 to 0x7FFFFFFFFFFF). Populates MEMORY_BASIC_INFORMATION with BaseAddress, RegionSize, State, Protect. Filters for MEM_COMMIT (0x1000) and readable protect flags (0xEE bitmask).

use_cases.name: Read Memory Region
use_cases.description: Copies committed+readable region bytes into a ctypes string buffer. Only proceeds if VirtualQueryEx indicates readable state.

use_cases.name: Match Pattern in Memory Buffer
use_cases.description: Applies compiled YARA rule to raw memory bytes. On match, extracts offset and matched_data from inst.instances[0].

use_cases.name: Format Match Context
use_cases.entry_point: format_llm_context(data, offset, base_addr, length=64)  |  hex context string
use_cases.description:
Two output modes: (1) llm_mode=True → format_llm_context() returns dict {address, offset, hex, ascii, hit_pos}; (2) default → returns 'Addr: 0x...
Hex: ...' string with context_size bytes around match.

use_cases.name: Perform Differential Address Scan
use_cases.description: Advanced use case for locating dynamic fields: full scan → store address set → switch UI state → ReadProcessMemory on stored addresses → filter changed/unchanged → converge to unique address. Uses raw k32.ReadProcessMemory directly.

key_conditions_guards: mbi.State == 0x1000 (MEM_COMMIT) AND mbi.Protect & 0xEE (readable) — required before ReadProcessMemory
h_proc == NULL → early return error (cannot open higher-integrity or system process)
next_addr <= curr_addr → break (prevents infinite loop on address overflow)
mode='auto': is_hex_pattern() decides hex vs string rule; mode='hex'/'string' forces type
data_flow: pid + pattern → build_rules() → YARA rules object
match offsets → format_llm_context() OR hex string → results list
results list → JSON output (llm_mode) OR match count (default)
security_notes: This is a process memory scraping tool targeting Windows. It extracts runtime credentials, UI state (e.g., WeChat active session), and arbitrary in-memory data from other processes. Requires PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION + PROCESS_VM_READ on target PID. The SOP explicitly describes extracting session titles and dynamic fields from messaging apps (WeChat/Weixin.exe).
